name: Azteca Restaurant

notes: The current College Park ordering catalog contains 103 rendered forms, 95 unique vendor item identifiers, and 94 canonical products after deterministic duplicate consolidation.
All three frozen rows reconcile: two exact products and one current named variant. Ninety-one current products were absent from the frozen catalog.
Post-repair recheck pins ninety-four unique canonical products in the source order across all nineteen current menu categories.
All three frozen rows reconcile exactly once; eight repeated vendor identifiers and the repeated Picadera presentation are consolidated without losing evidence.
The owner homepage establishes identity and links to a same-domain FOX Ordering menu, but the menu content is vendor infrastructure and is not promoted to restaurant-issued authority.

Sixty-five products contain narrow direct positive ingredient or unavoidable fish/shellfish signals; twenty-nine remain unavailable. No complete matrix, negative assurance, or cross-contact policy was found.
Sixty-five products retain narrow direct positives from linked-menu text and twenty-nine remain unavailable.
No wheat/gluten inference, negative assurance, or cross-contact claim is published from incomplete menu wording.
The frozen three-row repair used a different restaurant domain, omitted ninety-one current products, and overstated wheat/gluten for Grilled Quesadilla from flour-tortilla wording.
Eight repeated vendor item identifiers and the duplicated Plato Picadera Especial presentation require canonical consolidation.
The hash-pinned parser preserves source categories, descriptions, configurable products, and both Picadera vendor presentations.
The generated snapshot contains exactly ninety-four products with zero official-source promotions.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Captured the current restaurant homepage and its linkage to the College Park ordering surface.
notes: The homepage supplies identity and linkage, not an allergen matrix.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Captured the full current FOX Ordering catalog with item identifiers, categories, descriptions, and configurable presentations.
notes: Same-domain hosting does not change the vendor platform's authority tier; silence is not negative evidence.

findings.summary: The frozen catalog contains only three of ninety-four current canonical products.
findings.resolution: Restored all ninety-one omitted current products and pinned the complete ninety-four-product catalog.

findings.summary: All frozen rows cite a different restaurant domain and label partial menu descriptions as official ingredient evidence.
findings.resolution: Replaced the stale domain and classified every product as restaurant-linked menu evidence or unavailable.

findings.summary: The frozen Grilled Quesadilla promotes flour-tortilla wording to wheat and gluten despite no explicit source disclosure.
findings.resolution: Removed unsupported wheat/gluten and retained only the directly supported milk signal.

repairs.summary: Verified the complete catalog boundary, deterministic duplicate handling, linked-vendor authority, direct-positive semantics, and empty cross-contact arrays.

notes: Terra low was escalated to Sol medium; coordinator review corrected the platform authority and exact deduplication boundary.
Focused catalog/reconciliation tests passed 4/4 and the generated snapshot regression passed 1/1.
Generated counts: 94 total; 65 restaurant-linked menu positives; 29 unavailable; zero official; zero wheat/gluten; zero may-contain.
